raw_response:
```python
expected = {"000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"}
observed = set(counts.keys())
assert observed == expected, f"Expected all 8 outcomes, got {observed}"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"Total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

target = shots / 8
tolerance = max(2, 0.15 * target)
for bitstring in expected:
    c = counts.get(bitstring, 0)
    assert abs(c - target) <= tolerance, (
        f"Outcome {bitstring} count {c} not approximately uniform around {target}"
    )
```

```python
expected_states = {"000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"}
observed_states = set(counts.keys())
assert observed_states == expected_states, f"Expected all 8 outcomes, got {observed_states}"

expected = shots / 8
tolerance = max(0.1 * shots, 5)

for state in expected_states:
    c = counts.get(state, 0)
    assert abs(c - expected) <= tolerance, (
        f"State {state} count {c} not approximately uniform; expected about {expected}"
    )
```

```python
expected_states = {"000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"}
observed_states = set(counts.keys())
assert observed_states == expected_states, f"Expected all 8 outcomes, got {observed_states}"

expected_prob = 1 / 8
tolerance = metadata.get("tolerance", 0.08) if isinstance(metadata, dict) else 0.08

for state in expected_states:
    p = counts[state] / shots if counts[state] > 1 else counts[state]
    assert abs(p - expected_prob) <= tolerance, f"State {state} probability {p} not within {tolerance} of {expected_prob}"
```

```python
observed = {str(k): int(v) for k, v in counts.items()}
expected_states = {"000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"}

assert set(observed.keys()) == expected_states, f"Expected all 8 outcomes, got {set(observed.keys())}"

total = sum(observed.values())
assert total == shots, f"Total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

expected = shots / 8
tolerance = max(2, 0.2 * expected)

for state in expected_states:
    c = observed[state]
    assert abs(c - expected) <= tolerance, f"State {state} count {c} not approximately uniform around {expected}"
```

```python
expected = {"000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"}
observed = set(counts.keys())
assert observed == expected, f"Expected all 8 three-qubit outcomes, got {observed}"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"Total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

expected_count = shots / 8
tolerance = max(0.1 * shots, 5)  # allow typical sampling noise
for bitstring in expected:
    c = counts.get(bitstring, 0)
    assert abs(c - expected_count) <= tolerance, (
        f"Outcome {bitstring} count {c} not approximately uniform around {expected_count}"
    )
```

```python
expected_states = {"000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"}

for state in expected_states:

    assert abs(prob - expected_prob) <= tolerance, (

    )
```

```python
expected_states = {"000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"}
observed_states = set(counts.keys())

assert observed_states == expected_states, f"Expected all 8 outcomes, got {observed_states}"

expected_prob = 1.0 / 8.0
tolerance = 0.08

total_prob = sum(counts.values())
assert abs(total_prob - 1.0) < 1e-6, f"Counts should be normalized, got total {total_prob}"

for state in expected_states:
    p = counts[state]
    assert abs(p - expected_prob) <= tolerance, (
        f"Outcome {state} has probability {p}, expected about {expected_prob}"
    )
```

```python
expected = ["000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"]
total = sum(counts.get(k, 0) for k in expected)
assert total == shots

expected_prob = 1 / 8
tolerance = 0.08

for outcome in expected:
    assert outcome in counts, f"Missing outcome: {outcome}"
    prob = counts[outcome] / shots
    assert abs(prob - expected_prob) <= tolerance, f"Outcome {outcome} has probability {prob}, expected about {expected_prob}"
```

```python
expected_states = ["000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"]
total = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in expected_states)
assert total == shots, f"Expected total counted shots {shots}, got {total}"

expected = shots / 8
tolerance = max(2, 0.2 * expected)

for state in expected_states:
    value = counts.get(state, 0)
    assert value > 0, f"Missing outcome: {state}"
    assert abs(value - expected) <= tolerance, (
        f"Outcome {state} not approximately uniform: got {value}, expected about {expected}"
    )

assert len([state for state in expected_states if counts.get(state, 0) > 0]) == 8
```

```python
expected_states = ["000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"]
observed = {state: counts.get(state, 0) for state in expected_states}

assert set(counts.keys()) == set(expected_states), f"Expected exactly 8 three-qubit outcomes, got {sorted(counts.keys())}"

total = sum(observed.values())
assert total == shots, f"Total counts {total} do not match shots {shots}"

expected = shots / 8
tolerance = max(2, 0.2 * expected)

for state, value in observed.items():
    assert value > 0, f"Missing outcome {state}"
    assert abs(value - expected) <= tolerance, (
        f"Outcome {state} count {value} not approximately uniform around {expected}"
    )
```

```python
expected = {"000", "001", "010", "011", "100", "101", "110", "111"}
observed = set(counts.keys())
assert observed == expected, f"Expected all 8 outcomes, got {observed}"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total > 0, "Counts are empty"

target = total / 8
tolerance = max(0.1 * total, 0.05 * shots)

for bitstring in expected:
    c = counts.get(bitstring, 0)
    assert abs(c - target) <= tolerance, (
        f"Outcome {bitstring} count {c} not approximately uniform around {target}"
    )
```
